title: Paepalanthus dendroides
taxonomy: Paepalanthus dendroides is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. The classification of Paepalanthus dendroides places it within the genus Paepalanthus and the kingdom Plantae. This species is further grouped into the phylum Angiospermophyta and the class Liliopsida. The family Eriocaulaceae is a family of plants that are commonly known as pipeworts, and Paepalanthus dendroides is one of the species within this family. The classification of Paepalanthus dendroides is an important aspect of understanding its characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Paepalanthus dendroides, also known as "Pirá-paraíba", is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Eriocaulaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
